title: Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 635 Posts
organization: Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, New Delhi
department: Human Resource Development (HRD) Department, Punjab & Sind Bank
short_info: Punjab & Sind Bank is recruiting 635 Apprentice posts with Regular Graduation qualification, last date 06 June 2026.

application_fee.sc_fee: Rs. 100/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges
application_fee.general_fee: Rs. 200/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges
application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

qualification.nationality: Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal or Bhutan / Tibetan Refugee (came before 01-01-1962) / Person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable.
qualification.education_qualification: Regular Graduation in any discipline from a recognized University/Institute or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government.
qualification.experience: Candidates with work experience of 1 year or more after educational qualification are NOT eligible.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the respective cut-off dates. Only candidates with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals are eligible to apply through the Bank's website. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District and must not submit more than one application.

vacancy_details.Description: Locomotor Disability (OA, OL, BL, OAL), Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy
Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness
Combination of disabilities among VI, HI, OC, ID categories including Deaf-Blindness

vacancy_details.District: Central, East, New Delhi, South, West
Ambala, Gurugram, Karnal, Kurukshetra, Panchkula, Panipat, Sirsa
Amritsar, Barnala, Bathinda, Faridkot, Firozepur, Gurdaspur, Hoshiarpur, Jalandhar, Ludhiana
Agra, Allahabad, Bareilly, Gautam Buddha Nagar, Ghaziabad, Kanpur Nagar, Lucknow, Mathura, Meerut, Mirzapur, Muzaffarnagar, Pilibhit, Saharanpur, Varanasi
24 Paraganas North, 24 Paraganas South, Howrah, Kolkata

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Combination of disabilities among VI, HI, OC, ID categories including Deaf-Blindness

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 12,300/- per month (no daily allowance / conveyance / travelling allowance)

selection_process: Selection for engagement of apprentices will be merit-based on HSC/10+2 marks. No written examination is conducted. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility
and medical fitness. In case of a tie in marks
candidates will be ranked by age in descending order within their respective category
Stage 1 - Merit List (Based on HSC / 10+2 Marks)
Candidates who fulfill minimum eligibility criteria will be placed in a merit list in descending order of their 10+2 marks
and Category-wise. No written examination is conducted for this recruitment. Candidates must select their preferred State and District in the application itself. In case of equal marks
tie-break is by age in descending order within the respective category

Shortlisted candidates will be intimated through email only and must appear for document verification at their own expense. Original documents along with self-attested photocopies must be produced. Candidates failing to produce originals will not be allowed to proceed further.
Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank.

physical_eligibility.details: The competition level for PSB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. With only 635 seats spread across 29 States/UTs, the vacancy count is significantly lower than comparable bank apprenticeship drives such as SBI. Selection is purely merit-based on 10+2 marks without any written examination, which means candidates with high HSC scores - especially from states like Uttar Pradesh (100 seats), Punjab (90 seats), Bihar (40 seats), Maharashtra (30 seats), and Haryana (50 seats) - will face intense competition within their district and category. | Since there is no written exam, the effective competition depends entirely on the candidate's 10+2 percentage and age as a tie-breaker. Attrition at later stages will occur through local language proof verification, document verification, and medical fitness, which will reduce the final eligible pool. Candidates who do not have proof of studying the local language at 8th/10th/12th level are at a disadvantage as they must demonstrate local language proficiency separately.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online through the Bank's website (https://punjabandsind.bank.in/) only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must mandatorily complete 100% registration on both the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/). Candidates must also note down their Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal as it is required while applying.
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:
Recent passport-size colour photograph (to be signed across by the candidate on printing)
Signature with black ink on white paper (Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be accepted)
Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink

Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink - text: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at time of online application)
Image files must be in .jpg / .jpeg format; scanned documents must be in PDF format only
Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Joining:
Xth / Date of Birth certificate in support of Date of Birth
Printout of application form
All certificates and mark sheets of educational qualification from Standard Xth, XII, and Graduation onwards
Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format for SC/ST candidates
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format, valid as on date of joining (issued within one year prior to joining); OBC Caste Name must tally with Central Government list
Income and Asset Certificate in prescribed format issued by competent authority, valid EWS certificate for the financial year, for EWS category candidates
Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board for PwBD candidates
Age relaxation proof with valid documents (e.g., District Magistrate certificate for 1984 riot-affected persons)

Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Permanent Driving License / Voter’s Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Aadhaar / E-Aadhaar / Identity Card issued by recognised College/University / Gazetted Officer certificate) - bearing the same name as on the application / call letter. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are NOT valid.
Two passport-size photographs similar to the one used during portal registration
Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

NO DOCUMENT SHOULD BE DIRECTLY SENT TO THE BANK BEFORE OR AFTER THE PROCESS. Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to the Bank. Candidates must appear for document verification at their own expense.
Apply Online at: https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ → Home Page → Recruitment → Relevant Notification
For queries: Email [email protected] | All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only.
important_instructions: THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of the Bank and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for Bank employees. The Bank shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to any Apprentice who has completed the period of training, nor shall the Apprentice be obligated to accept employment. The Apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District, and not more than one application should be submitted. In case of multiple applications, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and fees for other registrations will stand forfeited. Mandatory registration with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying on the Bank's website is required. Candidates who had training or job experience of one or more years after completion of educational qualifications shall not be eligible for engagement as apprentice. OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General/UR. Change of address / email ID / phone number / caste / category will not be entertained after submission of the application. Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be acceptable. The signature must be consistent across all documents and correspondence. No daily allowance, conveyance allowance or travelling allowance will be paid to Apprentices. No overtime is permitted. Apprentices are entitled to only 1 casual leave per month (maximum 4 at a time). The Contract of Apprenticeship shall terminate automatically on expiry of the 12-month training period. A non-performing apprentice will be given 7 days' notice. An apprentice wishing to terminate early must give 15 days' prior notice to the Bank. Apprentices must devote their entire time exclusively to the Bank and shall not engage in any other commercial business, service, or advisory activity during the engagement period. Apprentices must not disclose any financial information, data, concepts or internal communications relating to the Bank's business to any unauthorized person. Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification. Any resultant dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of the Courts situated at Delhi. Bank reserves the right to change/add/cancel the selection process or cancel the entire process at any stage without notice or refund of application fees. All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only - candidates should visit regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is a fresh notification for 635 Apprentice posts under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The drive is open to Regular Graduates aged 20–28 years (with relaxations) who wish to undergo 12‑month on‑the‑job training in Bank branches across 29 States/UTs.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in PSB Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The Bank has announced 635 training seats for Apprentices, spread across 29 States/UTs including Uttar Pradesh (100), Punjab (90), Bihar (40), Haryana (50), and Maharashtra (30). The vacancies are distributed category‑wise (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR) with horizontal reservation for PwBD.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must: Be Indian citizen or fall under specified foreign‑national categories. Hold a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized university by 06‑06‑2026. Be 20–28 years as on 01‑05‑2026 (with relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/widows/1984‑riot‑affected). Have 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals. Not be PG‑qualified, no ≥1 year work experience after graduation, and not previously/currently undergoing apprenticeship under the Apprentices Act.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit and relaxation for PSB Apprentice exam 2026?
faq.answer: The age band is 20–28 years as on 01‑05‑2026 for General/EWS candidates. Relaxation is given as: SC/ST: +5 years OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer): +3 years PwBD: +10 years Widows/divorced/separated women: up to 35 (UR/EWS), 38 (OBC), 40 (SC/ST).

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for PSB Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: There is no written exam; selection is purely merit‑based on HSC/10+2 marks: State‑wise, District‑wise, Category‑wise merit list on 10+2 marks. Document verification (originals) for shortlisted candidates. Medical examination to confirm fitness. In case of a tie, older age is the tie‑breaker within category, state, and district.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend and training duration?
faq.answer: Selected Apprentices will get a monthly stipend of ₹12,300 for a 12‑month training period (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM), with no daily/conveyance or travel allowance. Apprentices are not treated as Bank employees and have no right to permanent absorption.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for PSB Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: SC/ST/PwBD: ₹100 + taxes + payment‑gateway charges. General/EWS/OBC: ₹200 + taxes + payment‑gateway charges. Fees are non‑refundable and must be paid online.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are required for PSB Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Mandatory documents include: Xth/XIIth/Graduation mark sheets and certificates Caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC‑NCL/EWS) PwBD disability certificate, age‑relaxation proof, Aadhaar, and valid ID proof (PAN, Passport, DL, etc.; Ration Card or Learner’s DL not accepted). Candidates must also upload photograph, signature, thumb impression, and handwritten declaration while applying.

faq.question: Q9. Is this recruitment a permanent job or just training?
faq.answer: This is only a 12‑month apprenticeship under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Apprentices are not employees of the Bank and do not get Bank‑employee benefits. The Bank has no obligation to offer permanent employment after training, and apprentices cannot claim absorption.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must: Complete 100% profile on NAPS and NATS portals and note the NATS Enrollment ID. Visit punjabandsind.bank.in → Recruitment → Apprentice 2026. Fill the online form, upload scanned documents (photo, signature, thumb impression, handwritten declaration), and pay the fee. Applications are online only; last date is 06‑06‑2026.
